programs.name: Oregon Health Plan (OHP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides comprehensive healthcare coverage including medical care, dental services, mental health treatment, prescriptions, vision care, and emergency services for low-income seniors. It also pays for long-term care services for eligible individuals who meet income and resource requirements.[1][2]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 65+ and disabled individuals meeting specific income and asset limits.[1][2]
programs.benefit_value: Free or low-cost health coverage; long-term care services in home or facility; expanded dental benefits as of 2023.[1][2]

programs.name: OPI-M (Medicaid-funded)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers home and community-based long-term care services for adults needing help with daily activities and health-related tasks. Funded by Medicaid, it allows eligible individuals to receive free services to stay in their homes.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Adults with limited income/resources needing home-based care; income up to $5,217/month (1 person, 2025), assets up to $94,523.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Free in-home services including personal care and health tasks; higher eligibility limits than regular OHP.[2]

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Not explicitly detailed in Oregon-specific sources; federally administered program providing comprehensive medical and social services to eligible seniors. Allows frail seniors to live safely in the community with coordinated care.
programs.who_its_for: Nursing facility-eligible seniors aged 55+ who can live safely in the community with services.
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive care including primary care, hospitalization, prescription drugs, and social services; value varies by provider.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs
programs.what_it_does: Helps pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and cost-sharing through programs like QMB, SLMB, QI; counseled via SHIBA. Provides financial assistance to low-income Medicare beneficiaries to reduce out-of-pocket costs.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare enrollees meeting income/resource criteria; accessible via OregONEligibility portal.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Covers Part A/B premiums (up to $185+/month), deductibles, coinsurance; exact amounts based on federal standards.

programs.name: SNAP Food Benefits
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly food assistance benefits to purchase groceries and nutritional support. Helps low-income seniors maintain adequate nutrition without financial burden.[1][6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors, available via OregONEligibility; income-based eligibility.[1][6]
programs.benefit_value: Monthly EBT benefits averaging $200+ per person depending on household size/income; supplements food costs.

programs.name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP) / Oregon Energy Assistance Program (OEAP)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP offers grants for energy bills and heating repairs; OEAP prevents utility disconnections for at-risk households. Tackles utility costs through provider grants and emergency aid.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors with income at or below 60% state median income.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Grants for bills/repairs; emergency aid to prevent shutoffs; distributed via 30+ partner agencies statewide.[1]

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Not explicitly detailed in results; federally funded program improving energy efficiency in low-income homes via insulation, sealing, and repairs. Reduces energy costs and improves home safety for seniors.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors; priority for elderly, disabled.
programs.benefit_value: Free home upgrades saving 10-30% on energy bills; health/safety improvements.

programs.name: Senior Health Insurance Benefits Assistance (SHIBA)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free, unbiased, confidential counseling on Medicare options, coverage, and savings programs. Helps seniors navigate enrollment, benefits, and Medicare Savings Programs.[4][5]
programs.who_its_for: Medicare enrollees aged 65+, new or current beneficiaries seeking advice.[4][5]
programs.benefit_value: Free counseling services; assistance accessing thousands in premium/cost savings annually.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors, providing over 2 million meals yearly. Supports nutritional needs and reduces food insecurity for isolated older adults.[1][5]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound or disabled seniors; available via local providers like Council on Aging.[1][5]
programs.benefit_value: Free or low-cost home-delivered meals (1-2/day); community dining options.

programs.name: Caregiver Support Programs
programs.what_it_does: Not explicitly named; supports family caregivers via respite, training, and resources through Aging and Disability Services. Helps caregivers manage stress and provide better care to seniors.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors/disabled adults eligible for long-term services.
programs.benefit_value: Respite care, counseling, training; value in hours of relief care (varies).

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Not detailed in results; federal program offering part-time paid training jobs in community service to unemployed low-income seniors. Promotes skill-building and transition to unsubsidized employment.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage training positions (up to 20 hrs/week); work experience and stipends.

programs.name: Legal Aid Services of Oregon (LASO)
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal services for low-income seniors on issues like benefits, housing, and consumer rights. Guides complex retirement and public benefit decisions.[1][4]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 60+ facing legal issues.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Free legal representation/advice; resolves disputes saving thousands in potential costs.

programs.name: Long-term Care Ombudsman
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities, investigating complaints and ensuring quality care. Protects rights of seniors in nursing homes and assisted living.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors in long-term care facilities and their families.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy and complaint resolution; improves facility conditions statewide.

programs.name: Elderly Rental Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides rental assistance to help low-income seniors afford housing and avoid displacement. It targets elderly individuals to support stable living arrangements.[1]
programs.whats_unique: Oregon-specific housing program for seniors, distinct from federal Section 8 or general rental aid, emphasizing elderly needs.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Rental subsidies; amount based on income and housing costs (specific figures not detailed in sources).

programs.name: Oregon Senior Free Tuition Audit Program
programs.what_it_does: Allows seniors to audit college classes tuition-free on a space-available basis. Universities like Oregon State University and University of Oregon waive fees for non-credit course participation.[2]
programs.whats_unique: State-specific tuition waivers at public universities for senior auditing, not universally available in all states.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Free auditing of classes (tuition waived); online options may be available.

programs.name: Health Related Social Needs Benefits (HSRN)
programs.what_it_does: Provides extra help with housing, food, or climate-related needs like air conditioners for health-impacted individuals. It supports those with complex health needs, homelessness risks, or life transitions.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Members of Oregon Health Plan or Health Share of Oregon with complicated health needs or related vulnerabilities.
programs.whats_unique: Oregon-specific enhancement to state health plans, integrating social supports like climate aid directly with medical coverage.[4]
programs.benefit_value: Targeted aid for housing, food, or items like AC units; value varies by assessed needs.
